summary: Keep WTS workflow-start state reviewable across the Workbench BFF and React stream fixtures.
rationale: The BFF exposes workflow-start state and reason codes in the Workbench conversation contract. The PR must update generated schema, typed React API tests, stream-view tests, and shared fixtures together so each stacked PR can pass Workbench Contract CI against its own parent.
verification: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_workbench_static_frontend.py tests/test_react_workbench_cutover_gate.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_agent_workbench_contract.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
CI=1 pnpm --dir apps/web-react exec vitest run src/lib/stream/agentStream.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamReducer.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamView.test.ts --run
uv run --group dev python tools/check_pr_governance.py --base codex/wts-06-workbench-actions
deletion_targets: Workflow-start BFF fields that only become typed on the frontend after a later schema-sync PR
Stream-view tests that ignore workflow-start state changes emitted by the BFF contract
Shared Workbench fixtures whose conversation object omits workflowStartState and workflowStartReasonCode
risks: Workflow-start state must reflect the durable BFF projection without starting duplicate workflow runs.
Reason codes must stay public and recruiter-safe.
React stream handling must treat these fields as product state, not developer debug output.
